items.name: CIA Triad
items.desc: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability — three pillars of information security; foundational to every course.

items.name: DAD Triad
items.desc: Disclosure, Alteration, Denial — the negative counterpart of CIA; what attackers achieve.

items.name: AAA (Authentication, Authorization, Accounting)
items.desc: Identity-and-access foundation; verify who, what they can do, and what they did.

items.name: Defense-in-Depth
items.desc: Layered security controls so a single failure does not compromise the system.

items.name: Zero Trust Architecture
items.desc: Never trust, always verify; micro-segmentation, JIT access, ABAC, mTLS — Lab 5 Helios assessment.

items.name: Risk = Threat × Vulnerability × Impact
items.desc: Foundational risk equation introduced in Lesson 08 and Week 2 of VM.

items.name: Vulnerability vs Threat vs Risk
items.desc: Vulnerability = weakness; Threat = potential adverse process; Risk = probability/loss from exploit.

items.name: Attack Vector vs Attack Surface
items.desc: Vector = path/technique; Surface = sum of exposed entry points (BYOD, M365, web portals).

items.name: DIKW Knowledge Pyramid
items.desc: Data → Information → Knowledge → Wisdom — analyst must process raw data into actionable wisdom.

items.name: Intelligence Cycle (6 phases)
items.desc: Planning & Targeting → Collection → Processing → Analysis & Production → Dissemination → Evaluation/Feedback.

items.name: Collection Management Framework (CMF)
items.desc: Structured approach to define collection methods based on Intelligence Requirements.

items.name: MITRE ATT&CK (Enterprise)
items.desc: Globally accessible matrix of real-world adversary tactics & techniques; foundation of hunting, detection, modeling. Used heavily in Lab 3.

items.name: MITRE ATT&CK Navigator
items.desc: Web tool for layering/heatmapping technique coverage per actor; Moe produced an SVG layer for Semiconductor APT (Lab 3).

items.name: Lockheed Martin Cyber Kill Chain
items.desc: 7 stages: Recon → Weaponization → Delivery → Exploitation → Installation → C2 → Actions on Objectives.

items.name: Diamond Model of Intrusion Analysis
items.desc: Adversary–Capability–Infrastructure–Victim relationships; integrates with MITRE.

items.name: STRIDE
items.desc: Microsoft threat-modeling: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Info Disclosure, DoS, Elevation of Privilege.

items.name: DREAD
items.desc: Risk rating: Damage, Reproducibility, Exploitability, Affected users, Discoverability.

items.name: PASTA
items.desc: Process for Attack Simulation & Threat Analysis — 7-stage risk-centric methodology.

items.name: LINDDUN
items.desc: Privacy threat modeling: Linkability, Identifiability, Non-repudiation, Detectability, Disclosure, Unawareness, Non-compliance.

items.name: OWASP Threat Modeling
items.desc: Decompose → Determine & Rank → Countermeasures (using STRIDE mitigations or OWASP ASF).

items.name: Pyramid of Pain
items.desc: Hash → IP → Domain → Network/Host artifacts → Tools → TTPs (most painful for adversary).

items.name: Data Flow Diagrams (DFDs)
items.desc: Visual model of system components, data flows and trust boundaries; foundational to STRIDE.

items.name: Trust Boundaries / Trust Levels
items.desc: Conceptual lines separating different privilege levels (Trusted/Trustworthy/Known/Unknown).

items.name: Attack Trees
items.desc: Tree representation of attack goals (root) and steps to achieve them (leaves).

items.name: Microsoft Threat Modeling Tool
items.desc: Free STRIDE-based threat modeler.

items.name: OWASP Threat Dragon
items.desc: Free open-source threat modeling tool.

items.name: IriusRisk
items.desc: Commercial threat-modeling platform with free tier.

items.name: SD Elements (Security Compass)
items.desc: Commercial requirements + threat modeling.

items.name: Cairis
items.desc: Open-source risk-driven design platform.

items.name: Threagile
items.desc: Open-source YAML-based threat modeling.

items.name: ThreatModeler
items.desc: Commercial automated threat-modeling tool.

items.name: NIST CSF 2.0
items.desc: 6 functions (Govern/Identify/Protect/Detect/Respond/Recover); applied in Helios assessment Lab 5.

items.name: NIST SP 800-30
items.desc: Guide for Conducting Risk Assessments.

items.name: NIST SP 800-37
items.desc: Risk Management Framework (RMF) lifecycle.

items.name: NIST SP 800-39
items.desc: Managing Information Security Risk.

items.name: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5
items.desc: Catalog of security & privacy controls (AC-2/3/4, SI-4, SC-7 cited in Lab 1).

items.name: NIST SP 800-61
items.desc: Computer Security Incident Handling Guide.

items.name: NIST SP 800-115
items.desc: Technical Guide to Information Security Testing & Assessment.

items.name: NIST SP 800-150
items.desc: Guide to Cyber Threat Information Sharing.

items.name: ISO 27001 / 27002 / 27005
items.desc: ISMS, controls, risk management — referenced across courses.

items.name: ITSG-33
items.desc: Government of Canada IT Security Guidance, Annex 3A control catalogue.

items.name: PCI DSS
items.desc: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard; segmentation of cardholder environment (Lab 3 in VM).

items.name: HIPAA
items.desc: US health-information privacy/security rule.

items.name: GDPR
items.desc: EU General Data Protection Regulation.

items.name: GLBA
items.desc: Gramm-Leach-Bliley Safeguards Rule for financial customer data (Helios Lab 5).

items.name: FedRAMP
items.desc: US federal cloud authorization program (Helios assessment).

items.name: FFIEC Guidelines
items.desc: US Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council guidance.

items.name: SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria (CC1.0–CC9.0)
items.desc: AICPA TSP Section 100 — 9 common-criteria categories applied in Helios mapping.

items.name: AICPA TSP Section 100
items.desc: Trust Services Criteria source document.

items.name: Computer Ethics Institute — Ten Commandments of Computer Ethics
items.desc: Ethics framework introduced in Lesson 08.

items.name: Utilitarian / Rights / Common Good Approaches
items.desc: Three ethical lenses for cyber decisions.

items.name: ALE / SLE / ARO / EF / AV
items.desc: Asset Value × Exposure Factor = SLE; SLE × Annualized Rate of Occurrence = ALE; phishing case-study walkthrough.

items.name: Risk Treatments (Accept/Avoid/Transfer/Mitigate)
items.desc: Four standard treatment options.

items.name: NIST Risk Management Framework (RMF)
items.desc: Prepare/Categorize/Select/Implement/Assess/Authorize/Monitor lifecycle.

items.name: Risk Register / Heat Map
items.desc: Structured documentation; severity-by-likelihood matrices used in Lab 4 VA.

items.name: Risk Residue
items.desc: Acceptable residual risk that cannot be eliminated cost-effectively.

items.name: Strategic Threat Intelligence
items.desc: Non-technical, high-level for C-suite (CEO/CFO/CIO/CISO/CSO/COO); whitepapers, policy docs, sector-targeting trends.

items.name: Tactical Threat Intelligence
items.desc: Detailed TTPs for architects/sysadmins; identifies attack vectors and detection guidance.

items.name: Operational Threat Intelligence
items.desc: Granular view of distinct active campaigns; supports patching priorities.

items.name: Technical Threat Intelligence
items.desc: Time-sensitive IOCs (IPs, URLs, hashes) for SOC and IR teams.

items.name: OSINT (Open-Source Intelligence)
items.desc: Public-access sources: web, social media, news, public databases & videos.

items.name: HUMINT (Human Intelligence)
items.desc: Direct human interaction — interviews, debriefs, social engineering, insider recruitment.

items.name: SIGINT (Signals Intelligence)
items.desc: Intercepted comms and electronic signals (NSA / CSE).

items.name: IMINT (Imagery Intelligence)
items.desc: Visual/satellite imagery (NGA).

items.name: MASINT (Measurement & Signature Intelligence)
items.desc: Scientific/technical data (DIA).

items.name: SOCMINT (Social-Media Intelligence)
items.desc: Subset of OSINT focused on Twitter/Facebook/LinkedIn/Instagram.

items.name: DRPS (Digital Risk Protection Services)
items.desc: Combines automated tools and HUMINT to detect underground threats.

items.name: Surface / Deep / Dark Web
items.desc: Indexed vs un-indexed vs Tor-encrypted web layers.

items.name: Shodan
items.desc: Search engine for internet-connected devices; banner-grabs services.

items.name: Maltego
items.desc: Data-mining and link-analysis platform for OSINT graphs.

items.name: Pipl
items.desc: Online identity/people search engine.

items.name: Wayback Machine
items.desc: Internet Archive — historical snapshots of web pages.

items.name: WHOIS / Domain Lookup
items.desc: Domain registration records; first-stop OSINT for domains/IPs.

items.name: Foto Forensics
items.desc: Image forensics — EXIF, error-level analysis, hidden content.

items.name: OSINT Framework
items.desc: Curated catalog of OSINT resources by category.

items.name: UrlScan.io
items.desc: Free URL sandbox showing DOM, screenshots, registrar, Cisco Umbrella rank.

items.name: Abuse.ch (project)
items.desc: Swiss research project hosting MalwareBazaar, Feodo Tracker, SSLBL, URLhaus, ThreatFox.

items.name: MalwareBazaar
items.desc: Database for sharing/searching malware samples (hashes).

items.name: Feodo Tracker
items.desc: Tracks botnet C2 (Emotet, Dridex, TrickBot, QakBot, BumbleBee, Pikabot).

items.name: SSL Blacklist (SSLBL)
items.desc: Malicious SSL certs and JA3 fingerprints associated with botnets.

items.name: URLhaus
items.desc: Database of malware-distribution URLs with ASN stats.

items.name: ThreatFox
items.desc: Shared IOC database (ioc:IP:port syntax).

items.name: PhishTool
items.desc: Email-triage tool — renders email, parses headers, extracts URLs/attachments.

items.name: Cisco Talos Intelligence
items.desc: IP/domain/file reputation lookup; WHOIS, OrgName.

items.name: VirusTotal
items.desc: Multi-engine file/URL/hash scanner with first-submission history.

items.name: AlienVault OTX
items.desc: Open Threat Exchange community feed.

items.name: AbuseIPDB
items.desc: IP reputation database fed by user reports.

items.name: GreyNoise
items.desc: Internet-noise classifier — separates targeted vs scanner traffic.

items.name: Censys / BinaryEdge / DNSdumpster / crt.sh
items.desc: Passive recon and certificate transparency search engines.

items.name: MaxMind GeoIP database
items.desc: Geo-location IP database used with Wireshark End-Point GeoIP.

items.name: MISP
items.desc: Malware Information Sharing Platform — open-source TIP.

items.name: OpenCTI
items.desc: Open-source Threat Intelligence platform.

items.name: STIX / TAXII
items.desc: Structured Threat Info eXpression / TAXII transport for sharing IOCs.

items.name: ThreatConnect
items.desc: Commercial TIP that aggregates feeds and connects SIEM/EDR/SOAR.

items.name: CrowdStrike Falcon
items.desc: EDR + threat intel; lightweight sensor with AI/behavioral analytics.

items.name: Google Threat Intelligence (Mandiant + VT)
items.desc: Google + Mandiant + VirusTotal combined feed.

items.name: Mandiant
items.desc: IR firm; source for UNC threat designations.

items.name: Secureworks
items.desc: TIP/IR firm — UNC4841 (Salt Typhoon) designations.

items.name: Cyber Threat Alliance (CTA)
items.desc: Sharing consortium (Palo Alto, Symantec, Fortinet, etc.) — VPNFilter case.

items.name: ISACs / ISAOs
items.desc: Industry/sector sharing organizations (IT-ISAC since 2000).

items.name: Anomali
items.desc: Threat-intel platform mentioned across course.

items.name: Indicator of Compromise (IOC)
items.desc: Reactive evidence of intrusion (IPs, hashes, registry keys, log entries).

items.name: Indicator of Attack (IOA)
items.desc: Proactive marker — real-time malicious behavior (lateral movement, C2 patterns).

items.name: TTPs (Tactics, Techniques, Procedures)
items.desc: Top of Pyramid of Pain — hardest for an adversary to change.

items.name: Threat Hunting (Proactive)
items.desc: Hypothesis-driven iterative search assuming breach has occurred; goal is shorter dwell time.

items.name: Hypothesis-driven Investigation
items.desc: Based on adversary TTPs; check whether behaviors are present in the environment.

items.name: Intel-based Investigation
items.desc: Catalog of known IOCs/IOAs from tactical intel.

items.name: Custom-based Hunting
items.desc: Geolocation, prior breaches, ML-driven anomaly hunting.

items.name: Dwell Time
items.desc: Time between adversary breach and detection.

items.name: MTTD / MTTR / MTOC / MTOE / MTOR
items.desc: Mean times to Detect/Respond/Onboard/Engage/Recover — measured in IR plans.

items.name: Script Kiddies
items.desc: Low-skill attackers using pre-written exploits; 2024 Russian Matrix DDoS botnet example.

items.name: Hacktivists
items.desc: Social/political/ideological motivation — DDoS, doxing, defacement; NoName057(16), Mr. Hamza.

items.name: Cybercriminals (Black Hats)
items.desc: Profit-driven phishing/ransomware/credential theft; FACTOR $10M, CDK Global $25M ransom.

items.name: Nation-State Adversaries
items.desc: State-sponsored (China/Iran/Russia/DPRK); zero-days, multi-month dwell time.

items.name: Insider Threats
items.desc: Privileged employees/contractors — 2020 Twitter Bitcoin scam, RCMP Cameron Ortis, Cisco $2.4M VM deletion.

items.name: Shadow IT / Rogue IT
items.desc: Unsanctioned IT/cloud/AI use bypassing policy — Samsung ChatGPT leak.

items.name: Gray Hat Hackers
items.desc: Between white and black hat — limited malicious intent.

items.name: White Hat / Ethical Hackers
items.desc: Authorized researchers performing scoped pen tests.

items.name: Advanced Persistent Threat (APT)
items.desc: Sophisticated long-term groups; 5 stages: Gain/Foothold/Deepen/Lateral/Look-Learn-Remain.

items.name: APT28 (Russia)
items.desc: Russian APT — referenced in motivations.

items.name: APT31 / ZIRCONIUM / Judgment Panda (China)
items.desc: 14-year cyber-espionage; spearphishing link (T1566.002), discovery (T1082).

items.name: APT34 (Iran)
items.desc: Iranian APT example.

items.name: APT43 / Kimsuky / Sparkling Pisces (DPRK)
items.desc: Credential-harvesting; KLogEXE keylogger, FPSpy backdoor.

items.name: Salt Typhoon / UNC2286 / Ghost Emperor (China)
items.desc: MSS-affiliated; Lab 1 case study; targeted US Congressional email & telecoms.

items.name: NoName057(16)
items.desc: Pro-Russian hacktivist DDoS group — 40+ Canadian orgs Dec 2024.

items.name: Pioneer Kitten (Iran)
items.desc: 2024 Iranian APT against US critical infrastructure.

items.name: Semiconductor APT (Lab 3)
items.desc: Suspected China-based; 2018-19 espionage on Taiwanese semi firms; SkeletonKeyInjector + Cobalt Strike.

items.name: Cobalt Strike
items.desc: Commercial red-team C2 framework; Lab 3 APT case study.

items.name: SkeletonKeyInjector (d3d11.dll)
items.desc: Injects skeleton key into DC NTLM/RC4 path; combines Mimikatz + Dumpert via direct syscalls.

items.name: Mimikatz
items.desc: Credential-dumping toolkit — source of skeleton-key code.

items.name: Dumpert
items.desc: LSASS-dump tool using direct syscalls.

items.name: Reflective Loader Shellcode
items.desc: Loads Cobalt Strike beacon directly to memory; broken PE header for evasion.

items.name: Modified RAR (RecordedTV.ms / vmware.log)
items.desc: One-byte-patched RAR 3.60 used to archive data before exfil.

items.name: Log4j / Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228)
items.desc: Java RCE via JNDI lookups in log messages — ThreatConnect TIP case.

items.name: VPNFilter
items.desc: 500K+ router botnet; CTA-coordinated detection (Palo Alto/Symantec/Fortinet).

items.name: Conti / LockBit / REvil / Ryuk
items.desc: Major ransomware families referenced.

items.name: WannaCry / NotPetya / EternalBlue / Stuxnet
items.desc: Famous worms / nation-state malware referenced as historical context.

items.name: OSI Model (7 layers)
items.desc: Physical/Data Link/Network/Transport/Session/Presentation/Application — each layer has distinct attacks/defenses.

items.name: TCP/IP Model
items.desc: Link/Internet/Transport/Application — stack that the internet actually runs on.

items.name: Ethernet (802.3)
items.desc: L2 frame format; MAC source/dest, EtherType — analyzed in hex panes (Lab Week 4).

items.name: ARP (Address Resolution Protocol)
items.desc: Resolves IP→MAC; ARP spoofing/cache poisoning is a MITM vector.

items.name: IPv4 / IPv6
items.desc: Network-layer addressing; IPv4 header analyzed field-by-field in Week 4.

items.name: TCP
items.desc: Connection-oriented L4; 3-way handshake (SYN/SYN-ACK/ACK), Seq/Ack, FIN/ACK abnormality detection.

items.name: UDP
items.desc: Connectionless L4; analyzed in Week 3 lecture.

items.name: ICMP / ICMPv6
items.desc: Diagnostic protocol; echo request/reply; ICMP analysis Week 3.

items.name: DHCP
items.desc: Dynamic Host Config Protocol; DHCP capture analyzed in Lab 3 (dhcp.pcapng).

items.name: DNS
items.desc: Domain Name System; DNS query/response analyzed in Lab 3 (DNS.pcapng).

items.name: HTTP / HTTPS
items.desc: Web protocols; clear-text vs TLS-encrypted.

items.name: FTP / SFTP
items.desc: File-transfer protocols; clear-text vs SSH-tunneled.

items.name: SSH
items.desc: Secure shell — encrypted remote admin (port 22).

items.name: Telnet
items.desc: Clear-text remote shell — captured in Lab 3 (telnet.pcap) showing exposed credentials.

items.name: SMTP / IMAP / POP3
items.desc: Email protocols.

items.name: SMB / NetBIOS
items.desc: Windows file sharing/name services.

items.name: RDP
items.desc: Remote Desktop Protocol — common lateral-movement vector.

items.name: SNMP / MIB / OID
items.desc: Simple Network Management Protocol; UDP 161 (agent), 162 (trap); MIB tree, OID identifiers; RFC 3418.

items.name: NTP
items.desc: Network Time Protocol; critical for log correlation.

items.name: STP / RSTP / MSTP
items.desc: Spanning Tree — rogue-switch attack vector (Week 1 anomaly example).

items.name: VLAN / VTP / 802.1Q
items.desc: L2 segmentation; VLAN hopping attacks.

items.name: 802.11 (Wi-Fi)
items.desc: Wireless LAN — referenced in monitoring.

items.name: BGP / OSPF / RIP / EIGRP
items.desc: Routing protocols (mentioned in network infrastructure context).

items.name: Wireshark
items.desc: GUI packet analyzer — Welcome screen, capture interfaces, packet list / details / bytes panes; Capture Filters and Display Filters; Export Packet, Merge, Conversation, Flow Graph, Expert Information.

items.name: tcpdump
items.desc: Command-line packet capture — `tcpdump -i any -c50`, `-eni`, `-D --list-interfaces`, `-w file.pcap`, `-r file.pcap`, `-nXX`. apt-get install tcpdump.

items.name: WinDump
items.desc: Windows port of tcpdump using Winpcap.

items.name: tshark
items.desc: Command-line Wireshark; same dissectors, scriptable.

items.name: mergecap
items.desc: Wireshark companion to merge multiple pcap files.

items.name: editcap
items.desc: Wireshark companion to slice / filter saved captures.

items.name: NetworkMiner
items.desc: Forensic packet analyzer — extracts files/credentials/sessions from PCAPs.

items.name: Zeek (Bro)
items.desc: Network-security monitor — high-level connection logs (conn.log, http.log, dns.log).

items.name: Snort
items.desc: Open-source IDS/IPS — signature-based rule engine.

items.name: Suricata
items.desc: Multi-threaded IDS/IPS — Snort-compatible rules + Lua scripting.

items.name: Security Onion
items.desc: All-in-one NSM/SOC distro (Zeek, Suricata, ELK, TheHive). VM provided as `CyberOps Security Onion.ova`.

items.name: CyberOps Workstation VM
items.desc: Cisco-provided lab VM bundled with Wireshark, Python, etc.

items.name: Kali Linux
items.desc: Penetration-testing distro used in labs (apt for tcpdump etc.).

items.name: Parrot OS
items.desc: Alternative pen-test distro used in CST8803 labs.

items.name: ntopng
items.desc: Real-time network-traffic monitor & flow analyzer.

items.name: pfSense
items.desc: Open-source firewall/router; Lab 8 dedicated lab.

items.name: MaxMind GeoIP
items.desc: Used with Wireshark End-Point GeoIP for geo-location.

items.name: Capture filter (BPF)
items.desc: Berkeley Packet Filter — applied before capture; e.g. `host 10.10.1.53`, `port 80`, `tcp and not host X`.

items.name: Display filter
items.desc: Wireshark expression language — `ip.addr == X`, `tcp.port == 80`, `http.request.method`, `tcp.flags.syn==1`, `dns.qry.name`, `arp.opcode`.

items.name: ip.addr / ip.src / ip.dst
items.desc: Display filter for IPv4 source/destination.

items.name: tcp.port / udp.port
items.desc: Filter by TCP/UDP port.

items.name: tcp.flags / tcp.flags.syn / tcp.flags.fin
items.desc: Detect handshake or anomalous flag combinations (SYN+FIN).

items.name: http.request / http.response
items.desc: Show HTTP requests/responses; Lab 5 web traffic.

items.name: dns.qry.name / dns.flags.response
items.desc: DNS query/response filtering for Lab 3.

items.name: arp.opcode
items.desc: Detect ARP request (1) vs reply (2); ARP spoofing identification.

items.name: Logical operators (and / or / not)
items.desc: Compose complex filter expressions.

items.name: Bookmark Filters
items.desc: Save commonly used filters in Wireshark for re-use.

items.name: Promiscuous mode
items.desc: NIC accepts all frames, not just those addressed to MAC; required to see other-host traffic.

items.name: SPAN / Port Mirroring
items.desc: Switch feature copying packets from one port to a monitor port.

items.name: Network TAP
items.desc: Passive hardware splitter — taps fiber/copper without disrupting traffic.

items.name: PCAP / PCAPNG
items.desc: Standard packet-capture file formats — read/write by Wireshark/tcpdump.

items.name: Full Packet Capture (FPC)
items.desc: Capturing entire payload (snaplen 65535) vs headers only.

items.name: Hex pane analysis
items.desc: Read hex bytes against frame/header field definitions (Week 4 lab).

items.name: Expert Information panel
items.desc: Wireshark feature surfacing protocol anomalies/duplicates/retransmits.

items.name: Conversation / Flow Graph
items.desc: Wireshark Conversation list and Flow Graph views — link to display filter.

items.name: Endpoint GeoIP
items.desc: Resolve IPs to country/city using MaxMind DB.

items.name: Anomalous traffic detection
items.desc: Identify deviations from baseline — bandwidth spikes, P2P among PCs, rogue devices.

items.name: SNMP NMS workflow
items.desc: Manager queries Agent (port 161); Agent sends Traps to Manager (port 162); use MIB/OID to read CPU/RAM/interface state.

items.name: Rogue switch / root-bridge attack
items.desc: Connect a switch with lower priority to seize the STP root role and re-route traffic.

items.name: Rogue router attack
items.desc: Inject false routes; re-route traffic for sniffing.

items.name: Critical asset traffic monitoring
items.desc: Use Wireshark Conversation + display filters to verify which hosts contact DBs/web servers.

items.name: Packet save & evidence chain
items.desc: Save captures with `tcpdump -w` or Wireshark Save As; merge multiple files with mergecap.

items.name: Lab 1 — Wireshark interface tour
items.desc: Identify menu sections, packet list/details/bytes panes, basic capture.

items.name: Lab 2 — tcpdump basics
items.desc: Run capture, list interfaces, filter by host/port; promiscuous mode.

items.name: Lab 3 — Protocol decode (DHCP, DNS, ICMP, Telnet)
items.desc: Read pcaps `dhcp.pcapng`, `DNS (1).pcapng`, `icmp.pcapng`, `telnet.pcap` with Wireshark.

items.name: Lab 4 — Hex/IPv4 header analysis
items.desc: Map captured bytes to header fields (Version/IHL/TOS/TTL/etc.).

items.name: Lab 5 — Capture & display filters
items.desc: Build BPF capture filters and Wireshark display-filter expressions.

items.name: Lab 6 — Save/merge/conversation
items.desc: Save captures, merge files, use Conversation and Flow Graph.

items.name: Lab 7 — Network monitoring (SNMP/MIB/OID)
items.desc: Configure SNMP and inspect MIB tree.

items.name: Lab 8 — pfSense
items.desc: Configure pfSense firewall, build rules, capture traffic crossing pfSense.

items.name: Lab 9 — Detection/IDS
items.desc: Apply Snort/Suricata-style rules, monitor for anomalies.

items.name: Lab 10 — Comprehensive analysis
items.desc: Full PCAP investigation; anomaly write-up.

items.name: Assignment 1 — Header walk-through
items.desc: Analyze IPv4/TCP/UDP/ICMP headers in supplied PCAP.

items.name: Assignment 2 — Anomaly hunt
items.desc: Inspect `Assingment2_Zgho0001.pcapng` for anomalies and document.

items.name: Final Project — Forensic PCAP investigation
items.desc: 7 PCAP files (file01-file07.pcapng); produce a `Practical_Zgho0001.pdf` IR-style write-up.

items.name: Vulnerability Management Lifecycle
items.desc: Discover → Assess → Prioritize → Remediate → Verify → Report — recurring program, not a one-off.

items.name: Vulnerability Assessment vs Pentest
items.desc: VA = breadth (find weaknesses); Pentest = depth (exploit and prove impact).

items.name: VM Governance — Security Testing/Assessment/Audit
items.desc: Recurring testing → technical assessment → independent audit hierarchy.

items.name: NIST Control Types — Technical / Operational / Managerial
items.desc: Technical (FW, ACL, encryption); Operational (training, IR, physical); Managerial (policies, audits, risk plan).

items.name: Asset Inventory & Classification
items.desc: Lab 2 — building an Assets Table (xlsx) for VM scope.

items.name: Scope of Engagement
items.desc: Authorization & boundaries — never scan systems you don't own without written permission.

items.name: Vulnerability Assessment Policy Template
items.desc: Class Activity / Quiz 2 — produce VA policy document.

items.name: VA Interview Questionnaire
items.desc: Class Activity — interview professor as 'CISO' to gather scope/needs (used in Lab 4 prep).

items.name: Information States — At Rest / In Motion / In Use
items.desc: Storage / network / RAM-printer; defines applicable controls.

items.name: CVE (Common Vulnerabilities & Exposures)
items.desc: MITRE-assigned identifiers for publicly disclosed vulnerabilities.

items.name: CVSS (Common Vulnerability Scoring System)
items.desc: Base/Temporal/Environmental score 0-10; severity ratings None/Low/Med/High/Critical.

items.name: CWE (Common Weakness Enumeration)
items.desc: Categorical taxonomy of software weaknesses (e.g., CWE-79 XSS).

items.name: NVD (National Vulnerability Database)
items.desc: NIST repository linking CVE→CVSS, CWE, references.

items.name: Exploit-DB
items.desc: Public exploit code archive (Offensive Security).

items.name: CISA KEV (Known Exploited Vulnerabilities)
items.desc: CISA must-patch list referenced in remediation prioritization.

items.name: Nmap
items.desc: Network mapper — host discovery, port scan, service & OS detection; flags `-sS/-sT/-sU/-sV/-O/-A/-p`; XML/HTML/Greppable export.

items.name: Zenmap
items.desc: Official Nmap GUI.

items.name: Nmap Scripting Engine (NSE)
items.desc: Lua scripts for vuln/discovery/exploit; `nmap --script vuln`.

items.name: Hping / Hping3
items.desc: Custom packet crafter — TCP/UDP/ICMP probes, Smurf/Land/DOS/DDoS amplification testing.

items.name: Unicornscan
items.desc: Asynchronous port scanner — alternative to Nmap.

items.name: Megaping
items.desc: Windows multi-host ping/traceroute/scan utility.

items.name: netdiscover / arp-scan
items.desc: Layer-2 host discovery via ARP.

items.name: Ping / Traceroute / Pathping
items.desc: Built-in connectivity & path-tracing utilities.

items.name: nslookup / dig / host
items.desc: DNS query CLI tools.

items.name: ipconfig / ifconfig / hostname / hostnamectl / systeminfo
items.desc: Built-in OS information utilities.

items.name: netstat / ss
items.desc: Local socket/connection listing.

items.name: arp / arp -a / route print
items.desc: Inspect ARP and routing tables.

items.name: Nikto
items.desc: Web-server vulnerability scanner — outdated software, dangerous files.

items.name: OWASP ZAP
items.desc: Open-source web app pen-test proxy (alternative to Burp).

items.name: Burp Suite
items.desc: Web app proxy / scanner — used in Hacker101 / PortSwigger labs.

items.name: SQLMap
items.desc: Automated SQL-injection tester.

items.name: WHOIS / DNSdumpster / crt.sh
items.desc: Passive recon, certificate transparency.

items.name: Banner grabbing
items.desc: Extract service version strings from open ports; basis for OS fingerprinting.

items.name: OS fingerprinting
items.desc: Identify operating system from TCP/IP stack quirks (Nmap -O).

items.name: Nessus (Tenable)
items.desc: Industry-standard authenticated/unauthenticated VA scanner with plugin engine.

items.name: Tenable.io / Tenable.sc
items.desc: SaaS / on-prem Nessus management.

items.name: OpenVAS / Greenbone
items.desc: Open-source VA scanner — Nessus alternative.

items.name: Qualys
items.desc: SaaS VA platform.

items.name: Rapid7 Nexpose / InsightVM
items.desc: Commercial VA platform with risk-based scoring.

items.name: Tripwire
items.desc: File-integrity monitoring + VA.

items.name: Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management
items.desc: Built-in scanner for Microsoft 365 / Intune-managed devices.

items.name: Metasploit Framework
items.desc: Exploit framework — modules for exploits, auxiliaries, post-exploitation; Lab 3 vulnerability exploitation.

items.name: Penetration testing methodology
items.desc: Recon → Scan → Exploit → Post-exploit → Report.

items.name: Packet crafting attacks (Smurf, Land, DoS, DDoS)
items.desc: Hping3 demonstrations — amplification factors 1, n, n^2.

items.name: DoS / DDoS Lab 6
items.desc: Pre/post-flood Wireshark captures comparing throughput.

items.name: Exploit verification
items.desc: Lab 3 — confirm exploitability; capture screenshots of compromised systems.

items.name: OWASP Top 10
items.desc: Most-critical web-app vulnerabilities — referenced for IIS server findings (Lab 4).

items.name: SQL Injection
items.desc: Untrusted input concatenated into SQL — classic web vuln.

items.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
items.desc: Reflected/stored/DOM XSS injection.

items.name: CSRF / SSRF
items.desc: Cross-Site Request Forgery / Server-Side Request Forgery.

items.name: LFI / RFI / Path Traversal
items.desc: File-inclusion and ../ traversal — PortSwigger lab solved.

items.name: Broken Access Control / IDOR
items.desc: Vertical/horizontal privilege escalation; PortSwigger Apprentice labs.

items.name: Username Enumeration
items.desc: Different error messages reveal valid usernames.

items.name: 2FA Bypass
items.desc: Skipping the 2nd-factor page when subsequent endpoints don't enforce.

items.name: Brute Force / Credential Stuffing
items.desc: Automated guessing or use of breached credential lists; Helios Lab 5.

items.name: IIS Server vulnerabilities
items.desc: Lab 4 — discover & document weaknesses on Windows IIS targets.

items.name: WSUS / SCCM / MECM / Intune / Jamf
items.desc: Microsoft and Apple patch / configuration management products.

items.name: Patch Tuesday
items.desc: Microsoft monthly security update cadence.

items.name: Configuration baselines / hardening guides (CIS Benchmarks)
items.desc: CIS-style hardening for OS, browsers, cloud workloads.

items.name: Asset & vulnerability remediation plan
items.desc: Lab 6 — create remediation tickets with owners, deadlines, verification steps.

items.name: Lab 1 — Course intro & VM setup
items.desc: Configure Kali/Parrot, set up CSA271 lab network.

items.name: Lab 2 — Asset inventory + VA scope
items.desc: Build Assets Table (xlsx), define scope.

items.name: Lab 3 — Network diagram + scanning
items.desc: Drawio CSA271 network diagram; Nmap/Nessus scans.

items.name: Lab 4 — IIS Server vuln assessment
items.desc: Out-of-scope and in-scope IIS scans; document findings.

items.name: Lab 6 — Hping3 / DoS testing
items.desc: Pre/post-flood traffic captures; remediation ideas.

items.name: Lab 7 — Domain Controller credential vuln
items.desc: Identify dc_Credentials_Critical findings.

items.name: Quiz 2 / Class Activity — VA policy
items.desc: Author Vulnerability Assessment Policy + interview questionnaire.

items.name: Packet-filter firewall (Stateless)
items.desc: ACL on 5-tuple (src IP, dst IP, src port, dst port, protocol); Cisco router ACL, AWS NACL.

items.name: Traditional / Stateful firewall
items.desc: Maintains session table; inspects 5-tuple + state — Cisco ASA without IPS license.

items.name: Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW)
items.desc: L2-L7 stateful + App-ID, User-ID, Content-ID, decryption — Palo Alto/Fortinet/Checkpoint/Cisco Firepower.

items.name: Zone-Based Firewall
items.desc: Firewall interfaces grouped into security zones; policies enforced between zones.

items.name: Transparent / Bridge / Layer-2 Firewall
items.desc: 'Bump in the wire'; not seen as a router hop; useful to drop in without changing IP plan.

items.name: Application-Level Gateway (ALG)
items.desc: Firewall opens dynamic 'pinholes' for protocols negotiating ports (e.g., FTP).

items.name: 5-Tuple ACL (Cisco/AWS)
items.desc: Source IP, Destination IP, Source Port, Destination Port, Protocol.

items.name: 6-Tuple Session (Palo Alto)
items.desc: 5-tuple + Security-zone-pair; uniquely identifies bidirectional Palo Alto sessions.

items.name: c2s / s2c Flow Direction
items.desc: Palo Alto labels first flow client-to-server, return server-to-client.

items.name: Stateful ICMP/UDP pseudo-state
items.desc: Echo Request creates session entry; reply matched and entry removed.

items.name: Asymmetric Routing & Stateful FW issue
items.desc: Stateful FW drops SYN-ACK if SYN took different path; classic data-center pitfall.

items.name: Default-Deny vs Default-Permit
items.desc: Final implicit rule of a security policy — best practice = default deny.

items.name: HA Cluster (Active/Active, Active/Standby)
items.desc: Palo Alto supports HA clusters of up to 16 firewalls; Cisco/Juniper similar.

items.name: Geo-Clustering
items.desc: Geographically distributed firewall clusters supported by some vendors.

items.name: Redundant Interface Identifier (RII)
items.desc: Pair-of-interfaces identifier used in Cisco redundant FW HA.

items.name: Network Address Translation (NAT)
items.desc: 1:1 / many:1 / port-translation; SNAT, DNAT, PAT.

items.name: Port Address Translation (PAT)
items.desc: Many internal IPs share one public IP via different source ports.

items.name: Port Forwarding / Static NAT
items.desc: Map external IP:port → internal host.

items.name: DMZ / Semi-Trusted Network
items.desc: Internet-accessible network for public web/mail servers.

items.name: Enclave Network
items.desc: Higher-protection internal segment (extra FW/VPN/VLAN/NAC).

items.name: Trusted vs Untrusted Zone
items.desc: Internal vs external network designations.

items.name: Sinkhole
items.desc: Redirect malicious-domain traffic to a controlled IP for analysis/blocking.

items.name: Palo Alto Networks (PAN-OS)
items.desc: Course's primary NGFW platform; PAN-OS, App-ID, User-ID, Content-ID, WildFire, GlobalProtect.

items.name: Panorama (Palo Alto)
items.desc: Centralized management for up to 5,000 PAN firewalls.

items.name: PAN-OS Packet-Flow stages
items.desc: Initial Packet Processing → Pre-Policy → Application Identification → Security Policy → Content-ID → Forwarding.

items.name: PAN Virtual Wire (VWire)
items.desc: Drop-in transparent FW deployment binding two interfaces.

items.name: PAN Layer 2 / Layer 3 / Tap interfaces
items.desc: Different deployment modes — switch-like, routed, passive sniffing.

items.name: PAN HA Tunnel interfaces
items.desc: Logical interfaces for site-to-site VPN, GlobalProtect SSL/IPsec.

items.name: Cisco ASA / Cisco PIX / Firepower (FTD)
items.desc: Cisco firewalls; FTD is successor; transparent FW config `firewall transparent`.

items.name: Cisco IOS Zone-Based Firewall (ZBFW)
items.desc: Stateful FW on Cisco routers; successor of CBAC.

items.name: Fortinet (FortiGate)
items.desc: NGFW vendor referenced in course.

items.name: Check Point
items.desc: NGFW vendor.

items.name: Juniper SRX / NGFW
items.desc: NGFW vendor referenced.

items.name: AWS Security Groups / NACLs
items.desc: Cloud-native packet/stateful filters referenced in NGFW comparison.

items.name: Azure / GCP firewalls
items.desc: Cloud firewall references.

items.name: App-ID
items.desc: PAN feature identifying applications regardless of port/protocol/encryption.

items.name: User-ID
items.desc: Maps username (AD/LDAP/Kerberos) to traffic for identity-based policy.

items.name: Content-ID
items.desc: Threat-prevention engine — IPS, AV, anti-spyware, URL-filter, DLP.

items.name: WildFire
items.desc: PAN cloud-based malware sandbox.

items.name: GlobalProtect (SSL/IPsec VPN)
items.desc: PAN remote-access and always-on VPN.

items.name: Threat Prevention
items.desc: Combined IPS / AV / anti-spyware bundle.

items.name: URL Filtering
items.desc: Category-based web filtering on NGFW.

items.name: DNS Security / Sinkhole
items.desc: Inspect DNS, sinkhole malicious queries to controlled IPs.

items.name: SSL/TLS Decryption
items.desc: MITM TLS termination on FW for visibility/DLP.

items.name: Application-Level Gateway (ALG) on PAN
items.desc: Pinhole creation for FTP, SIP, etc.

items.name: IDS (Network/Host)
items.desc: Intrusion Detection System; Snort/Suricata/Bro/Cisco NGIDS — alerts only.

items.name: IPS (Network/Host)
items.desc: Intrusion Prevention System; inline, drops malicious traffic.

items.name: HIDS / HIPS
items.desc: Host-based detection/prevention.

items.name: NIDS / NIPS
items.desc: Network-based detection/prevention.

items.name: Snort rule syntax
items.desc: Action protocol src→dst (msg, content, sid, rev) syntax.

items.name: Suricata rule syntax
items.desc: Snort-compatible plus extended keywords.

items.name: YARA / Sigma rules
items.desc: YARA = malware identification; Sigma = vendor-agnostic SIEM rules.

items.name: SIEM (Security Information & Event Management)
items.desc: Aggregate logs, correlate, alert; Microsoft Sentinel, Splunk, ELK, Graylog, Wazuh.

items.name: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud
items.desc: SIEM used in Lab 2 firewall log ingestion (`sourcetype=cisco:asa`).

items.name: Splunk Search Processing Language (SPL)
items.desc: `stats count by event.code, event.action`, `dedup`, `eval port_risk=case(...)`, `timechart`.

items.name: ELK Stack (Elastic / Logstash / Kibana)
items.desc: Open-source SIEM alternative.

items.name: Graylog
items.desc: Open-source log management platform.

items.name: Wazuh
items.desc: Open-source XDR/SIEM (HIDS, FIM, vulnerability detection).

items.name: Microsoft Sentinel
items.desc: Azure-native SIEM/SOAR.

items.name: SOAR (Security Orchestration, Automation & Response)
items.desc: Tines, Cortex XSOAR, Splunk SOAR (Phantom).

items.name: EDR / XDR / MDR
items.desc: CrowdStrike, SentinelOne, Defender, Carbon Black, Cylance.

items.name: UEBA / UBA
items.desc: User & Entity Behavior Analytics — anomaly detection.

items.name: Common Event Format (CEF) / Syslog
items.desc: Standard log formats consumed by SIEM.

items.name: Sysmon
items.desc: Microsoft Sysinternals endpoint logging — process creation, network conn, file hash.

items.name: Mininet
items.desc: Network emulator running real Linux network stack on virtual hosts; Lab 1 lab.

items.name: OpenFlow
items.desc: SDN control-plane protocol used by Mininet/OVS.

items.name: Open vSwitch (OVS)
items.desc: Software switch used by Mininet; supports OpenFlow.

items.name: Mininet CLI commands (h1 ping h2, pingall, dpctl, link, iperf)
items.desc: Lab 1 hands-on Mininet.

items.name: VirtualBox / VMware / Hyper-V / ESXi / Proxmox
items.desc: Virtualization platforms used to run lab VMs.

items.name: Security Onion VM / CyberOps Workstation VM
items.desc: Provided OVAs for hands-on labs.

items.name: Active Directory / LDAP / Kerberos
items.desc: Windows directory services and authentication; User-ID source.

items.name: RADIUS / TACACS+
items.desc: Network device AAA — admin auth + 802.1X.

items.name: 802.1X
items.desc: Port-based network access control with RADIUS.

items.name: MFA / 2FA / TOTP / HOTP / FIDO2 / WebAuthn
items.desc: Multi-factor authentication primitives — discussed in Helios remediation.

items.name: SAML / OAuth 2.0 / OIDC
items.desc: Federated SSO and authorization standards (Helios OAuth-token issue).

items.name: PAM (Privileged Access Management) — CyberArk / BeyondTrust / HashiCorp Vault
items.desc: Secrets/privilege brokerage products.

items.name: Lab 1 — Mininet introduction + Initial FW config
items.desc: Bring up Mininet topology, run pings; configure baseline FW settings.

items.name: Lab 2 — Packet & Traditional Firewalls
items.desc: Build/test stateless ACL & stateful policies.

items.name: Lab 3 — Firewall hardening
items.desc: Initial firewall configuration tasks (PAN-OS).

items.name: Lab 4 — NAT & VPN basics
items.desc: Source-NAT, Destination-NAT, GlobalProtect demo.

items.name: Lab 5 — App-ID and traffic decryption
items.desc: Identify apps regardless of port; basic SSL decrypt.

items.name: Lab 6 — User-ID configuration
items.desc: Map AD users to IPs for identity policies.

items.name: Lab 7 — Security policies + logs
items.desc: Author multi-zone security rules; verify in Monitor logs.

items.name: Lab 8 — User-ID Security Policies
items.desc: Per-user/per-group access control rules.

items.name: Lab 9 — SIEM/log integration
items.desc: Forward FW logs to SIEM, build dashboards.

items.name: Plaintext / Ciphertext / Enciphering / Deciphering
items.desc: Core terminology of Stallings Crypto8e Ch. 1.

items.name: Cryptography vs Cryptanalysis vs Cryptology
items.desc: Encrypting/decrypting algorithms vs breaking them vs the umbrella discipline.

items.name: Symmetric / Conventional / Single-Key Encryption
items.desc: Same key for encryption and decryption — DES, AES, 3DES.

items.name: Asymmetric / Two-Key / Public-Key Encryption
items.desc: Public/private key pair — RSA, ECC, ElGamal, DH, DSA.

items.name: Block Cipher
items.desc: Operates on fixed-size blocks (typ. 128 bits).

items.name: Stream Cipher
items.desc: Operates bit-by-bit using XOR with keystream.

items.name: Hash Function (Cryptographic)
items.desc: Variable-input → fixed-length digest; one-way; collision-resistant.

items.name: Message Authentication Code (MAC)
items.desc: Symmetric-key cryptographic authentication tag — HMAC, CMAC.

items.name: Digital Signature
items.desc: Asymmetric integrity + non-repudiation; sign with private, verify with public.

items.name: Confusion vs Diffusion (Shannon)
items.desc: Confusion masks key↔ciphertext; diffusion spreads plaintext influence.

items.name: Kerckhoffs' Principle
items.desc: Security depends on key, not algorithm secrecy.

items.name: Caesar Cipher
items.desc: Monoalphabetic shift — `c = (p + k) mod 26`.

items.name: Monoalphabetic Substitution
items.desc: Arbitrary 26-letter permutation; broken by frequency analysis.

items.name: Playfair Cipher
items.desc: 5×5 matrix bigram cipher.

items.name: Hill Cipher
items.desc: Linear-algebra cipher using mod-26 matrix multiplication.

items.name: Vigenère Cipher
items.desc: Polyalphabetic — keyword length = period; broken by Kasiski/Friedman.

items.name: Vernam / One-Time Pad (OTP)
items.desc: True random key length ≥ message; perfect secrecy if key not reused.

items.name: Rail Fence Cipher
items.desc: Transposition — write zigzag and read row-by-row.

items.name: Row/Column Transposition
items.desc: Permutation cipher with key permuting columns.

items.name: Steganography
items.desc: Hide existence of message (LSB images, invisible ink) — distinct from encryption.

items.name: Cryptanalysis types
items.desc: Ciphertext-only, known-plaintext, chosen-plaintext, chosen-ciphertext, adaptive.

items.name: Brute-Force Attack
items.desc: Exhaustive key search; feasibility depends on key length.

items.name: Frequency Analysis
items.desc: Letter / bigram / trigram statistics to break monoalphabetic ciphers.

items.name: Linear Cryptanalysis
items.desc: Linear approximations of cipher rounds.

items.name: Differential Cryptanalysis
items.desc: Studies how input differences propagate to ciphertext.

items.name: DES (Data Encryption Standard)
items.desc: 56-bit key Feistel cipher; obsolete due to brute force.

items.name: Triple DES (3DES / TDEA)
items.desc: 3 DES applications with 2 or 3 keys; 168-bit key.

items.name: AES (Rijndael)
items.desc: 128-bit block; 128/192/256-bit keys; SP-network cipher.

items.name: Blowfish / Twofish
items.desc: Bruce Schneier ciphers — 64/128-bit blocks.

items.name: RC4 / RC5 / RC6
items.desc: Ronald Rivest stream/block ciphers (RC4 deprecated).

items.name: Feistel Cipher Structure
items.desc: Iterated round function with subkeys; basis of DES, Blowfish, etc.

items.name: Substitution-Permutation Network (SP-Network)
items.desc: Structure used by AES — alternating S-boxes and permutation layers.

items.name: ECB (Electronic Codebook)
items.desc: Independent block encryption; reveals patterns; not for general use.

items.name: CBC (Cipher Block Chaining)
items.desc: XOR previous ciphertext with current plaintext; needs IV.

items.name: CFB (Cipher Feedback)
items.desc: Self-synchronizing stream-mode of block cipher.

items.name: OFB (Output Feedback)
items.desc: Synchronous stream mode using IV chained through cipher.

items.name: CTR (Counter)
items.desc: Stream mode where counter is encrypted and XORed with plaintext; parallelizable.

items.name: GCM / CCM
items.desc: Authenticated-encryption (AEAD) modes — GCM uses GHASH; widely used in TLS 1.2/1.3.

items.name: Padding schemes (PKCS#7)
items.desc: Block alignment for ECB/CBC.

items.name: Initialization Vector (IV) / Nonce
items.desc: Per-message random value used to randomize ciphertext.

items.name: RSA
items.desc: Public-key cryptosystem based on factoring problem; encryption + signature.

items.name: Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC)
items.desc: Smaller-key alternative to RSA; ECDH, ECDSA.

items.name: Diffie-Hellman (DH) / ECDH
items.desc: Key-exchange protocol over insecure channel.

items.name: ElGamal
items.desc: Discrete-log based public-key encryption + signature.

items.name: DSA / ECDSA
items.desc: Digital Signature Algorithm and elliptic-curve variant.

items.name: Pre-Shared Key (PSK)
items.desc: Symmetric key shared out-of-band — VPN/Wi-Fi.

items.name: Session Key / Master Key / KEK
items.desc: Key hierarchy — short-lived session keys protected by KEK.

items.name: Kerberos / KDC
items.desc: Symmetric-key authentication server; ticket-granting service.

items.name: MD5
items.desc: 128-bit hash — broken; only for legacy.

items.name: SHA-1
items.desc: 160-bit hash — collisions found (Shattered).

items.name: SHA-2 (SHA-256, SHA-512)
items.desc: Current secure hash family used in TLS, code signing.

items.name: SHA-3 / Keccak
items.desc: Latest NIST hash standard (sponge construction).

items.name: HMAC
items.desc: Hash-based MAC — HMAC-SHA256 widely used.

items.name: CMAC
items.desc: Block-cipher-based MAC.

items.name: Rainbow Tables / Salting
items.desc: Pre-computed hash tables; salting defeats them.

items.name: Birthday Attack / Hash Collision
items.desc: Sqrt(2^n) attack on hash output space.

items.name: PKI (Public Key Infrastructure)
items.desc: Hierarchy of CA / sub-CAs / RAs / users; trust anchors.

items.name: X.509 v3 Certificates
items.desc: Standard digital-cert format; fields: Subject, Issuer, Validity, Public Key, Extensions.

items.name: Certificate Authority (CA) / Sub-CA
items.desc: Trust-issuing entity; root cert in OS trust store.

items.name: CRL / OCSP / OCSP Stapling
items.desc: Certificate revocation mechanisms.

items.name: PKCS#1 / #5 / #7 / #8 / #10 / #12
items.desc: Public-Key Cryptography Standards (RSA encoding, PBE, signed data, key storage, CSR, PFX).

items.name: TLS / SSL
items.desc: Transport-Layer Security; handshake using asymmetric → symmetric session keys.

items.name: IPsec / IKEv2
items.desc: Network-layer VPN encryption suite.

items.name: PGP / GPG / OpenPGP
items.desc: Pretty Good Privacy / GnuPG — email & file encryption with web of trust.

items.name: OpenSSL (Win64OpenSSL-3_0_13.exe)
items.desc: CLI for symmetric/asymmetric encryption, hashing, key generation, X.509 — used in Lab 2 confidentiality.

items.name: Gpg4win 4.3.1 / Kleopatra / GPA
items.desc: Windows GPG suite for PGP encryption, signing, key management.

items.name: GnuPG (gpg)
items.desc: Command-line OpenPGP implementation.

items.name: John the Ripper Jumbo (1.9.0-jumbo-1-win64)
items.desc: Password-cracking tool — wordlist, mask, incremental, MARKOV modes; readers for SSH, GPG, PDF, OFFICE, BITLOCKER, FILEVAULT2, LUKS, RACF, IBM AS400, KERBEROS, KEYRING, PWSAFE, AXCRYPT, KERIO, TEZOS, etc.

items.name: Notepad++ (npp.8.8.5)
items.desc: Hex-friendly text editor for handling cipher input/output.

items.name: Hashcat / HashCalc
items.desc: GPU-accelerated cracker / hash computation.

items.name: CyberChef
items.desc: Browser 'cyber Swiss-Army knife' — encode/decode/encrypt/decrypt by chaining recipes.

items.name: CrypTool 1 / 2
items.desc: Educational crypto suite for visualization of classical and modern algorithms.

items.name: ASCII / Hex / Base64
items.desc: Foundational text-encoding schemes used throughout course; ASCII Conversion Chart provided.

items.name: Man-in-the-Middle (MITM)
items.desc: Intercept and possibly alter messages between two parties.

items.name: Replay Attack
items.desc: Re-send valid captured message to gain unauthorized access; nonces/timestamps mitigate.

items.name: Birthday Attack
items.desc: Hash collision via probabilistic search.

items.name: Brute Force / Exhaustive Search
items.desc: Try every key; mitigated by larger key space.

items.name: Side-Channel Attacks
items.desc: Timing, power, EM analysis to leak key material.

items.name: Padding Oracle
items.desc: Exploit error responses to recover plaintext under CBC.

items.name: Course Intro / Ch. 1 — Information & Network Security
items.desc: Stallings 8e Chapter 1 — security attacks, services, mechanisms.

items.name: Ch. 3 — Classical Encryption Techniques
items.desc: Caesar, Vigenère, Hill, Playfair, OTP, Transposition.

items.name: Ch. 4/6/7 — Block Ciphers, Modes, Stream Ciphers (DES/AES/RC4)
items.desc: Symmetric cryptography; AES rounds, modes of operation.

items.name: Ch. 8 — Public-Key Cryptography
items.desc: RSA, DH, ECC fundamentals.

items.name: Lab 1 — Quiz on classical ciphers
items.desc: Solve hand-cipher quiz with screenshots.

items.name: Lab 2 — Confidentiality (OpenSSL)
items.desc: Encrypt/decrypt files with OpenSSL CLI; demonstrate symmetric/asymmetric workflows.

items.name: Security Operations Centre (SOC)
items.desc: Centralized monitor/detect/analyze/respond function.

items.name: SOC Tier 1 — Triage Analyst
items.desc: Alert monitoring via SIEM/EDR; first-line triage.

items.name: SOC Tier 2 — Incident Responder
items.desc: Containment, forensics, malware analysis.

items.name: SOC Tier 3 — Threat Hunter
items.desc: Proactive search using ATT&CK knowledge.

items.name: SOC Manager / Team Lead
items.desc: Tooling strategy + executive risk reporting.

items.name: Security Engineer / Automation Engineer
items.desc: Builds tools; writes Python/YAML/SOAR playbooks.

items.name: In-House SOC / Virtual SOC / Hybrid Co-Managed / MSSP
items.desc: Four operating models — control/cost tradeoffs.

items.name: Playbooks
items.desc: Step-by-step IR procedures (e.g., CEO ransomware response).

items.name: Incident Response Phases (NIST SP 800-61)
items.desc: Detection → Containment → Eradication → Recovery → Lessons Learned.

items.name: Autonomous SOC / Identity-as-Perimeter
items.desc: AI-resolved alerts; identity-centric defense.

items.name: DevOps / SecOps / DevSecOps
items.desc: Plan-Code-Build-Test-Release-Deploy-Operate-Monitor with security shifted left.

items.name: SAST / DAST / IAST / SCA
items.desc: Static/Dynamic/Interactive AST + Software Composition Analysis.

items.name: Dependency / IaC scanning
items.desc: Scan open-source libs and Terraform/CloudFormation for vulns.

items.name: Shift Left
items.desc: Catch security issues early; rule-of-10 cost growth in production.

items.name: Penetration Testing
items.desc: Authorized scoped attacker simulation; NDA + report.

items.name: Hacker101 CTF (HackerOne)
items.desc: Web/Crypto CTF — Moe scored 83 pts on Discussion 3.

items.name: TryHackMe
items.desc: Threat-Intelligence-Tools room used in Lab 4.

items.name: Hack The Box (HTB) Academy
items.desc: Cracking-into-HTB path: Web Requests, JavaScript Deobfuscation, Getting Started.

items.name: PortSwigger Web Security Academy
items.desc: Apprentice labs: Path traversal, IDOR, broken access, 2FA bypass, username enumeration.

items.name: CompTIA Security+ / CySA+ / PenTest+
items.desc: Industry-standard certs.

items.name: EC-Council CEH / CCNA Security / OSCP / SANS GIAC
items.desc: Advanced ethical-hacking certs.

items.name: Technical threat-assessment report
items.desc: Salt Typhoon Lab 1 — 9-page revision history, exec summary, threat profile, controls, IEEE references.

items.name: Incident-response narrative report
items.desc: Lab 2 firewall log — anomaly identification, smoking-gun visualization.

items.name: Threat-actor profile
items.desc: Lab 3 Semiconductor APT and Salt Typhoon profiles.
